name: drizzle-schema-definition-with-relations
description: Validate that the skill produces correct Drizzle schema definitions with proper table builders, column types, relations, type inference, and index declarations for a multi-entity domain.
input: Design a Drizzle ORM schema for a blog platform using PostgreSQL. The domain includes: users (email, display name, role), posts (title, content, status as draft/published/archived, author), comments (content, post, author), and tags with a many-to-many relationship to posts. Include type inference, relations, and indexes on foreign keys. Use pgTable and the relational query API conventions.

assertions.description: Tables must be defined using pgTable since the target is PostgreSQL. Using sqliteTable or mysqlTable would generate incompatible SQL.
Relation declarations must be present for the relational query API (db.query.*) to function. Missing relations cause incomplete query results.
Type inference must use $inferSelect and $inferInsert from the schema rather than manually defined interfaces, preventing type drift.
Foreign key columns must use .references() to create database-level constraints for referential integrity.
The post status (draft/published/archived) should use pgEnum to leverage PostgreSQL native enums for type safety at both database and TypeScript levels.
The many-to-many junction table (posts-to-tags) must have a composite primary key to prevent duplicate associations.

name: drizzle-migration-workflow
description: Validate that the skill produces a correct drizzle-kit migration workflow including configuration, generation, custom data migration, and deployment safety practices.
input: I have an existing Drizzle schema with a users table (id, email, name, created_at). I need to: (1) add a 'status' enum column (active, suspended, deleted) with default 'active', (2) add an 'organization_id' foreign key column that's nullable for now, (3) create the organizations table, and (4) write a data migration to set all existing users to 'active' status. Show the full drizzle-kit workflow: config, schema changes, generate, custom migration, and apply steps.

assertions.description: The workflow must reference drizzle-kit commands (generate, migrate, or push) since drizzle-kit is the migration tool for Drizzle ORM.
The workflow must use drizzle-kit generate (not push) to create migration files, since push is unsafe for production databases.
The status column must be defined as a pgEnum to leverage PostgreSQL native enums rather than plain varchar, ensuring database-level validation.
The data migration must be wrapped in a transaction to ensure atomicity — partial backfills leave the database in an inconsistent state.
The new status column must have a default value ('active') so that existing rows and future inserts without explicit status are handled correctly.
